observations: The Array.isArray narrowing pattern is idiomatic TypeScript for handling union types from Express 5's ParamsDictionary (string | string[]).
The fallback `rawRadarId[0] ?? ""` correctly handles the edge case of an empty array, producing an empty string that reduceLive() will reject with a proper error.

Two catch blocks use `err: any` (lines 367, 378) which violates the mission's 'No any types' convention. Should use `unknown` with `err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'fallback'`. However, this is pre-existing uncommitted code, not introduced by the worker's type fix edit.
The type narrowing correctly resolves TS2345: `string | string[]` is narrowed to `string` before being passed to reduceLive(radarId: string).
Runtime behavior is preserved: Express route params (`:radarId`) are always strings at runtime, so the Array.isArray branch is never hit in practice — this is purely a compile-time fix.
tsc --noEmit passed with zero errors after the fix (verified in handoff).

The commit scope includes two new Express route handlers (/api/submit-packet, /api/reduce-live/:radarId) that were present in the working tree but uncommitted prior to this feature. The type error was in the reduce-live handler. The worker correctly fixed only the type issue and committed the whole file.

No tests were added. The worker's justification is reasonable: the feature explicitly states 'Do NOT change the runtime behavior, only fix the type error so tsc --noEmit passes', making tsc itself the verification.
The skill's TDD procedure was deviated from, but the deviation is justified for a type-only fix where there is no runtime behavior change to test.

nonBlockingIssues.description: catch (err: any) violates 'No any types' convention. Should use `catch (err: unknown)` with `err instanceof Error` narrowing. Applies to both catch blocks at lines 367 and 378. This was pre-existing uncommitted code, not introduced by this fix.

The commit includes the /api/submit-packet route handler (lines 362-369) which is unrelated to the type error fix. This was pre-existing uncommitted code that got swept into the commit. Minor scope creep, but harmless since both routes were needed.

sharedStateObservations.description: Backend worker skill mandates 'Write failing tests first (TDD)' but doesn't carve out an exception for type-only fixes where tsc --noEmit is the verification. Worker's skillFeedback suggests adding this exception. This is a valid observation — pure type fixes (no runtime changes) should be exempt from TDD since the type checker IS the test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'TDD doesn't apply to pure type fixes — tsc --noEmit IS the test.' Feature description explicitly says 'Do NOT change the runtime behavior, only fix the type error so tsc --noEmit passes.'

sharedStateObservations.description: Express 5 types (@types/express-serve-static-core@5.1.1) define ParamsDictionary values as `string | string[]`, which causes type errors when passing req.params.* to functions expecting string. This is a known quirk that should be documented in library/environment.md so future workers don't spend time investigating.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows investigation of @types/express-serve-static-core@5.1.1 type definitions to understand why req.params.radarId was typed as string|string[]. The fix pattern is: `const raw = req.params.key; const val = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw[0] ?? '' : raw;`

sharedStateObservations.description: The 'No any types' coding convention isn't consistently enforced. The committed code has `catch (err: any)` in two new route handlers. Consider documenting the preferred error handling pattern: `catch (err: unknown) { const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'fallback'; }`
sharedStateObservations.evidence: orgs/riatzukiza/threat-radar-mcp/src/main.ts lines 367 and 378 use `catch (err: any)` despite AGENTS.md stating 'No any types'.

summary: The type fix is correct and minimal. The worker properly narrowed Express 5's `string | string[]` param type using Array.isArray before passing to reduceLive(). tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors. Runtime behavior is unchanged since Express route params are always strings. The commit includes two new route handlers (submit-packet and reduce-live) that were pre-existing uncommitted code — only the type narrowing in reduce-live was the actual fix. No blocking issues. Minor non-blocking observations: `err: any` in catch blocks should be `err: unknown`, and the backend-worker skill could document an exception for type-only fixes not requiring TDD.
